title: Psychoanalytical Interpretation of NGEby Cassiano
wikitext:
[]: Psychoanalytical Interpretation of NGE:by Cassiano             Quote Post by Sanada Yukimura » Mon Sep 27, 2010 5:01 pm Well as I've promised,finally here it is ,the promised speech that excerpts the essence of Evangelion and answers some questions (at the point of view of Psychoanalysis)-surely there are other interpretations of Eva but for me this rocks! (For now I need to take a nap(that translation rly took me hours),but please leave your impressions about this!And leave any comment to my mail box if there are phrases that needs to become more clear to understand! Think and have fun!) Does society need Adam or Lilith? One thing has noticed a lot by me when I revised Evangelion. Along the anime, characters are looking for the fatherlike figure or the motherlike figure [*1] .According to Freudian Psychology the only place where we really have felt safe and happy was in the mother’s uterus .The majority of people ‘s greatest trauma is to have been born,we left a good warm place where there were no complications to get physician’s slaps and to begin trying to understand everything about.(Refutable,but Evangelion uses Freudian Theory and let’s go into it). Well,according to this branch of psychoanalysis, there are two kinds of “energies” that control our development- the paternal and maternal-these words has nothing to do with the meaning of father or mother properly speaking ,because both father and mother can assume any one of the characteristics or even the two of them. The paternal one is rational,that protects us,but oppress us because of that.”I give you home,food and clean clothes,but you will have to follow my rules” The maternal one is emotional,that does not protect us,but caresses us .”Poor dear son,don’t cry because your papa became angry when you wanted to go out tonight,he just wants to protect you” For an efficient human development,we need those two energies acting in our life.If we have a family where both father and mother,or even the responsible for the children,treat us with a paternalistic,imposing “energy”,as a dictatorial government,we become victims who do everything to please our own parents and the society as a whole.We are Shinji. On the other hand if only the “maternal energy” manifests,without having anyone to protect us,we become people with no limit.As the ‘boys-of-the-building’ who burn indigenes [*2] and say in inner conflict say ”Sorry,I thought he was a mendicant”.We are Asuka(well,it seems she misses maternal energy,but her tutors were too benevolent to her;in fact I think she only misses her mother herself and not the maternal energy) Proceeding, the last episode elaborates,works well that idea and [*3] they show how the characters want to come back to uterus where everything was good.Shinji,naked,grabbing his legs, begins to disfigurate himself until he becomes a uterus,in one of the two last episodes. But the target that I want to attain is the following.According to that Freudian theory,the capitalist world is paternalist.And the Japanese society is even more,both culturally and modernly. In episode 24,Kaworu is decided to cause the third impact uniting himself with Adam.When he meets him,he finds out that he isn’t Adam,it’s Lilith.Then he suddenly realizes that people don’t want to change the world through rational imposition,domination,they miss caress,they miss love to the next one without asking anything in exchange,they want the mother. In a society as Brazilian’s,maybe it’s difficult to understand that, we live here in conflict because the world is paternal but our lifestyle is maternal.The foreigners who come here says about how friendly we are or they think ,because we hug,we are prosmicuous; that’s what they may say.The same way about the success of Orkut. But in Japanese society,where people asks apologies if they touch any part of the others’ body ,where the workers at the market have to take the change in a tray,it’s easy to understand why the angel(apostle)had pity when he saw Lilith where he expected Adam and why he decided that human race must survive. It’s very interesting to find out that Evangelion discourses about a world that judges itself ‘the man’ because of the democracy and the ending of religious obscurantisms,but it doesn’t realize what is the most basic thing to human being,the love. As stranger as it seems,Evangelion uses rationalist science to refute the own science in order to call up a idea known as sappy,and that's why it's our major problem. The illuminist rationalism have been so afraid of sentimentalism that caused the Middle Age,and that's why it has focused all its efforts to the seek of rational improvement.However,as so Joseph Campbell,a modern philosopher who ,among other stuff,has written The Power of Myth, said: “Conscience thinks that it’s itself the one who command all things.It is just a secondary organ of a total human being and it mustn’t take over the control.It must submit and assist the human nature to the body.When the conscience takes over the control what comes up is a Darth Vaider ,the man who passed to the intellectual side.He doesn't thinks and lives in human terms,he lives in terms of a system.” And that is exactly the implicit message of Evangelion ,even more present in the last 2 episodes when Shinji finds out he has a heart too. A message that is so much valid and,at my point of view,also very beautiful. A message that the world needs. CREDITS Written by Cassiano ,a guy who believes in love,at NGE community of Orkut, Translated and Adapted by “Sanada Yukimura”,Michaelis dictionary and Globalink Translator Pro If you have orkut account and know how to speak Portuguese click here *1=according to Freud idea of Oedipus’s Complex,learn it quickly seeing this(( http://courses.washington.edu/freudlit/Oedipus.Notes.html )) *2=curious incident that happened in our capital Brasília last year(2009),where 5 “sons of papa”(in fact sons of some politicians) has taken alcohol and fire in a indigen that was sleeping in a public square at high hours of night.Here is the case of the indian: (( http://pt.wikipedia.org/wiki/Galdino_Jesus_dos_Santos)) *3=it seems the author was a in delirium while he writes this ‘-‘.It was totally like a lit organized free association of ideas,no concern with structures ,but here he commited an orthographic mistake;it was supposed to be “e” that means “and” ,and not “é” that means the verb “is”.

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Mon Sep 27, 2010 10:38 pm To be sure, do you have the link to the original source page/manuscript?

[]:             Quote Post by Sanada Yukimura » Mon Sep 27, 2010 11:17 pm no problem soul assassin, I've justfixed some errors about meanings and structure. I modified his text a bit in order to be more understandable for all. ;] Try read again and let's see if u can understand,any problem tell me ok?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Sep 28, 2010 12:03 am Well, for me, I don't see anything in this article that's particularly enlightening or insightful. The idea of returning to the womb equaling comfort should be quite apparent to anyone who watches NGE, and while the concept is Freudian, in a sense, considering Anno associates this with the collective mother and unconsciousness relates more strongly (to me) with his concept of Collective Unconscious opposed to Freud. I'm not sure about the whole masculine/feminine energy angle. That in itself seems like a banal archetype present in an enormous amount of fiction.

[]:             Quote Post by Sanada Yukimura » Tue Sep 28, 2010 12:37 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: the collective mother and unconsciousness relates more strongly (to me) with his concept of Collective Unconscious opposed to Freud. I'm not sure about the whole masculine/feminine energy angle. That in itself seems like a banal archetype present in an enormous amount of fiction.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Sep 28, 2010 12:41 am Sanada Yukimura wrote: And he explains the message Anno tried to transmit as a possible solution or salvation or a reason for humans continuing exists in spite of everything...afterall don't u feel a little restricted by social manners and rigid education,don't u feel so much demand by your parents by the society?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Sep 28, 2010 9:03 am I think the article has some points right, but referring to controlling/supportive parenting styles as paternal and maternal "energies" seems rather odd, and even with the in-text disclaimer it's hard not to see this as antiquated sexism. I also have to agree with Jimbo that this seems too obvious to be worth writing about. afterall don't u feel a little restricted by social manners and rigid education,don't u feel so much demand by your parents by the society ?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Sep 28, 2010 11:52 pm NemZ wrote: Understanding one's motivations allows control over how and when those drives are acted upon rather than letting them constantly trip you up.

[]:             Quote Post by Sanada Yukimura » Wed Sep 29, 2010 12:36 am As stranger as it seems,Evangelion uses rationalist science to refute the own science in order to call up a idea known as sappy,ridiculously sentimental,and that's why this is our major problem. The illuminist rationalism have been so afraid of sentimentalism that caused the Middle Age,and that's why it has focused all its efforts to the seek of rational improvement(...)
